We have no other King than You—You took us out of Egypt, drowned our enemies, and at that time we sang Shirah to You. And You consistently answer our nation whenever they cry out to You for help.

segments.heText: מִמִּצְרַיִם גְּאַלְתָּנוּ יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ, וּמִבֵּית עֲבָדִים פְּדִיתָנוּ: כָּל בְּכוֹרֵיהֶם הָרָגְתָּ, וּבְכוֹרְךָ גָּאָלְתָּ, וְיַם סוּף בָּקַעְתָּ, וְזֵדִים טִבַּעְתָּ, וִידִידִים הֶעֱבַרְתָּ, וַיְכַסּוּ מַיִם צָרֵיהֶם, אֶחָד מֵהֶם לֹא נוֹתָר: עַל זֹאת שִׁבְּחוּ אֲהוּבִים וְרוֹמְמוּ אֵל, וְנָתְנוּ יְדִידִים זְמִירוֹת שִׁירוֹת וְתִשְׁבָּחוֹת, בְּרָכוֹת וְהוֹדָאוֹת לְמֶלֶךְ אֵל חַי וְקַיָּם: רָם וְנִשָּׂא, גָּדוֹל וְנוֹרָא, מַשְׁפִּיל גֵּאִים, וּמַגְבִּיהַּ שְׁפָלִים, מוֹצִיא אֲסִירִים, וּפוֹדֶה עֲנָוִים, וְעוֹזֵר דַּלִּים, וְעוֹנֶה לְעַמּוֹ בְּעֵת שַׁוְּעָם אֵלָיו: תְּהִלּוֹת לְאֵל עֶלְיוֹן בָּרוּךְ הוּא וּמְבֹרָךְ, מֹשֶׁה וּבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל לְךָ עָנוּ שִׁירָה בְּשִׂמְחָה רַבָּה, וְאָמְרוּ כֻלָּם:

segments.enText: מִמִּצְרַיִם גְּאַלְתָּנוּ יְהוָה—And at that time we sang Shirah to You. And You consistently answer our nation whenever they cry out to You for help. תְּהִלּוֹת לְאֵל עֶלְיוֹן—(When You split the sea and saved us from the Egyptians) Moshe and Bnei Yisrael sang Your praises and called out:

segments.heText: מִי כָמֹכָה בָּאֵלִים יְהוָה, מִי כָּמֹכָה נֶאְדָּר בַּקֹּדֶשׁ, נוֹרָא תְהִלֹּת עֹשֵׂה פֶלֶא:

segments.enText: מִי כָמֹכָה בָּאֵלִים יְהוָה—Among all the powers (powers of nature, powers of humans), who is equal to You, Hashem? מִי כָּמֹכָה נֶאְדָּר בַּקֹּדֶשׁ—Who is like You: powerful but with purity of purpose; נוֹרָא תְהִלֹּת עֹשֵׂה פֶלֶא—our songs praising You instill in us awe of Your wondrous deeds, which are all beyond our comprehension.

segments.heText: שִׁירָה חֲדָשָׁה שִׁבְּחוּ גְאוּלִים לְשִׁמְךָ עַל שְׂפַת הַיָּם, יַחַד כֻּלָּם הוֹדוּ וְהִמְלִיכוּ וְאָמְרוּ:

segments.enText: שִׁירָה חֲדָשָׁה—And at that time they all gave thanks, accepted Your sovereignty, and proclaimed:

segments.heText: יְהוָה יִמְלֹךְ לְעֹלָם וָעֶד:

segments.enText: יְהוָה יִמְלֹךְ לְעֹלָם וָעֶד—(Just as we have seen the rulership of Hashem here at the sea, so too for all future times) Hashem will reign forever and ever!

segments.heText: צוּר יִשְׂרָאֵל, קוּמָה בְּעֶזְרַת יִשְׂרָאֵל, וּפְדֵה כִנְאֻמֶךָ יְהוּדָה וְיִשְׂרָאֵל: גֹּאֲלֵנוּ יְהוָה צְבָאוֹת שְׁמוֹ קְדוֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵל: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה, גָּאַל יִשְׂרָאֵל:

segments.enText: צוּר יִשְׂרָאֵל—(And therefore we ask that just like You miraculously saved us then from the Egyptians and we sang Shirah, so too now) Hashem, our Redeemer, once again rise up and save us, and bring Mashiach as You promised. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything; גָּאַל יִשְׂרָאֵל—Who has redeemed Bnei Yisrael (from Egypt, and will redeem us in the future).

The idea of an עֹל—"yoke," as in the concept of עֹל מַלְכוּת—accepting the yoke of the Kingdom of Heaven, seems to carry an image of mitzvos being a heavy, difficult burden that we shlep around on our backs our whole life. True, at times mitzvos seem "heavy" and difficult, and we are obligated to persevere. But the choice of the term עֹל carries a profound message. As I heard from Rav Matisyahu Solomon, shlita, a yoke is simply a tool that allows the animal to do its job in the most efficient and easy way possible. How else could it pull the wagon if it did not have a yoke? So too, when we proclaim the Shema and publicly accept עֹל מַלְכוּת שָׁמַיִם, the yoke of the Kingdom of Heaven, we must realize that the mitzvos are perfectly crafted, precise tools that allow us to do OUR job here on earth! How would we manage without them?

The letters of the Shema that begin with ש-מ-ע are the same as the letters that spell the words עַל מַלְכוּת but backward! The message is that even when Torah laws and values seem "backward" to us—as if they make no sense—nevertheless we accept that in truth they are the tools for our success.

In the Torah, the pasuk of the Shema has the letters ע and ד (which spell עֵד—witness) enlarged. The very miracle of our survival—that after all these years Jews all over the world are united in their proclamation of the Shema—is witness to the truth of the Torah!

And now that we are firm in our conviction that Hashem has chosen us, has taken care of us, will take care of us, and is the only power Who controls all elements of our lives, we come before Him with our requests—so that we will have all we need in order to do our unique part in this great mission in the best way possible.
FAQ: Honestly, I don't really care that much about my unique mission in this world—I just daven here because I need stuff from Hashem. Is that so bad? ANSWER: Very honest question! See Appendix 8.

segments.heText: שְׁמֹנֶה עֶשְׂרֵה
segments.enText: OUR REQUESTS
Opening Statement

segments.heText: אֲדֹנָי, שְׂפָתַי תִּפְתָּח וּפִי יַגִּיד תְּהִלָּתֶךָ:

segments.enText: אֲדֹנָי, שְׂפָתַי תִּפְתָּח וּפִי יַגִּיד תְּהִלָּתֶךָ—(I am standing here because I do want to daven, create a relationship, and thank You for all the good You do for us—which is sometimes hard to see or understand—and I want to ask You for the help I need. But that does not come easy to me for so many reasons. Therefore, I am asking, please) Hashem, open my lips and help me express myself properly.
FAQ: Why am I asking Hashem for stuff? Hashem knows what I want, and He also knows what I really need. If I really need it and am supposed to have it, He would have given it to me!

FAQ: Why should I bother making requests from Hashem? I've asked Him many times before for stuff and He keeps on saying no! ANSWER: Both very crucial questions for a serious Ben Torah and Bas Yisrael!! See Appendix 1.
Section 1—Praises

segments.heText: אבות

segments.enText: Hashem took care of us from the beginning:

segments.heText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה, אֱלֹהֵינוּ וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵינוּ, אֱלֹהֵי אַבְרָהָם, אֱלֹהֵי יִצְחָק וֵאלֹהֵי יַעֲקֹב, הָאֵל הַגָּדוֹל, הַגִּבּוֹר וְהַנּוֹרָא, אֵל עֶלְיוֹן, גּוֹמֵל חֲסָדִים טוֹבִים, וְקוֹנֵה הַכֹּל, וְזוֹכֵר חַסְדֵי אָבוֹת, וּמֵבִיא גוֹאֵל לִבְנֵי בְנֵיהֶם לְמַעַן שְׁמוֹ, בְּאַהֲבָה:

segments.enText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה, אֱלֹהֵינוּ וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵינוּ—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything, You control everything, and have taken care of Bnei Yisrael throughout our history starting from the time of the Avos. You are all-powerful, You shower us with acts of helpful kindness (even though at times it is hard to see that), and everything is Yours. When dealing with us, You take into account the good deeds of our ancestors, and (everything You do for us is in order to) bring Mashiach to us, their descendants, even if we do not deserve it!
During Aseres Yemei Teshuvah add: (If you forgot—see Appendix 12:1)

segments.heText: זָכְרֵנוּ לְחַיִּים, מֶלֶךְ חָפֵץ בַּחַיִּים, וְכָתְבֵנוּ בְּסֵפֶר הַחַיִּים, לְמַעַנְךָ אֱלֹהִים חַיִּים:

segments.enText: זָכְרֵנוּ לְחַיִּים—Please, Hashem, remember us for life; מֶלֶךְ חָפֵץ בַּחַיִּים—for You are our King Who desires life; וְכָתְבֵנוּ בְּסֵפֶר הַחַיִּים—and write us in the book of life; לְמַעַנְךָ אֱלֹהִים חַיִּים—so that we may be able to continue to serve You and live up to our potential.

The terms מוֹשִׁיעַ, עוֹזֵר and מָגֵן represent three levels of help that we need in our lives—from the least to the most. עוֹזֵר—"help," is when we are on the right track, doing what we need to do in the best way possible, and we ask Hashem to please help us that all should work out, since nothing will come to fruition without His help. מוֹשִׁיעַ—"salvation," is when we are completely at a loss; we throw up our hands and say to Hashem, "Save me, I have no idea what to do next!" The third and highest level, מָגֵן—"protect," is when we think we're on the right track and think we know what we're doing, but in truth we are completely off base! We ask Hashem, "If I'm going in the wrong direction—מָגֵן—protect me from myself!"

segments.heText: מֶלֶךְ עוֹזֵר וּמוֹשִׁיעַ וּמָגֵן: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה, מָגֵן אַבְרָהָם:

segments.enText: מֶלֶךְ עוֹזֵר וּמוֹשִׁיעַ וּמָגֵן—You are our King, You have the power to help us through our difficulties, to save us (from our physical and spiritual enemies), and to protect us (from evil). בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything; מָגֵן אַבְרָהָם—You are the Protector of Avraham (and us, his descendants).

segments.heText: גבורות

segments.enText: Hashem is all-powerful:

segments.heText: אַתָּה גִּבּוֹר לְעוֹלָם, אֲדֹנָי, מְחַיֵּה מֵתִים אַתָּה, רַב לְהוֹשִׁיעַ:

segments.enText: אַתָּה גִּבּוֹר לְעוֹלָם, אֲדֹנָי—Hashem, Your power and rulership are permanent; You bring about Techiyas Hameisim (since our souls are eternal); and You have unlimited power to save us.
During the winter add:

segments.heText: מַשִּׁיב הָרוּחַ וּמוֹרִיד הַגֶּשֶׁם:
נֹצֵר חֶסֶד לָאֲלָפִים.

segments.enText: He extends the reward for mitzvos that a person does for two thousand generations (for the benefit of his descendants).

segments.heText: נֹשֵׂא עָוֹן.

segments.enText: He forgives deliberate sins.

segments.heText: וָפֶשַׁע.

segments.enText: He forgives rebellious sins.

segments.heText: וְחַטָּאָה.

segments.enText: He forgives unintentional sins.

segments.heText: וְנַקֵּה:

segments.enText: He cleanses those who do teshuvah and gives them a fresh start!

segments.heText: וְסָלַחְתָּ לַעֲוֹנֵנוּ וּלְחַטָּאתֵנוּ וּנְחַלְתָּנוּ: סְלַח לָנוּ אָבִינוּ כִּי חָטָאנוּ, מְחַל לָנוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ כִּי פָשָׁעְנוּ, כִּי אַתָּה אֲדֹנָי טוֹב וְסַלָּח וְרַב חֶסֶד לְכָל קֹרְאֶיךָ:

segments.enText: Please forgive our sins—and keep us as Your special and eternal nation—for You are full of kindness to all who call to You.
A SPECIAL TIME, SPECIAL REQUESTS

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ

segments.enText: On fast days (except Tishah B'Av) and during the Aseres Yemei Teshuvah, we add Avinu Malkeinu before Tachanun.

Is Hashem our kind and compassionate Father (אבינו) Who understands our failings, or is He our King (מלכנו) with demands and rules? The answer is—He is both! Hashem is our King, with a special mission for each of us, with rules and structures to help us maximize our spiritual potential to fulfill that mission. And we must never lose sight of our responsibilities and the end goal—those highest rungs of the ladder we climb in our avodas Hashem. Our decisions do have very serious consequences!

Yet, importantly, He is also our Father Who understands that at times we stumble, lose our footing, and get sidetracked from our goal. Through all this, He still supports us and gives us multiple opportunities to get back on track. On these special days, when we think about who we really are, what our mission is, what our real potential is, and take stock of our progress, we need to focus on both these aspects of our relationship with Him. By doing that, we will be worthy of Hashem giving us what we are asking for so that we can get back up and be the people He created us to be.
NOTE: The Hebrew of this tefillah is quite simple and straightforward. I urge you to say each line slowly in the original.

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ חָטָאנוּ לְפָנֶיךָ:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, we have sinned against You.

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ אֵין לָנוּ מֶלֶךְ אֶלָּא אָתָּה:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, (even though we may not be personally deserving, but please) we have no King besides You!

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ עֲשֵׂה עִמָּנוּ לְמַעַן שְׁמֶךָ:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, (even though we may not be personally deserving, but please) deal kindly with us, for the sake of Your Name (so that we will have the ability to fulfill our mission and sanctify Your Name).

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ חַדֵּשׁ עָלֵינוּ שָׁנָה טוֹבָה:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, initiate for us a good new year.

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ בָּרֵךְ עָלֵינוּ שָׁנָה טוֹבָה:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, bless us with a good year.

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ בַּטֵּל מֵעָלֵינוּ כָּל גְּזֵרוֹת קָשׁוֹת:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, cancel all harsh decrees concerning us (since they will hold us back from fulfilling our mission).

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ בַּטֵּל מַחְשְׁבוֹת שׂוֹנְאֵינוּ:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, cancel the plans of those who hate us.

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ הָפֵר עֲצַת אוֹיְבֵינוּ:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, defeat the schemes of our enemies.

segments.heText: אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ כַּלֵּה כָּל צַר וּמַשְׂטִין מֵעָלֵינוּ:

segments.enText: Our Father, our King, destroy all those who attack and oppress us.

segments.heText: חֲצִי קַדִּישׁ

segments.enText: יִתְגַּדַּל וְיִתְקַדַּשׁ שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא — (The person saying Kaddish proclaims our hope that) soon, in our lifetime, the entire world, which Hashem created precisely according to His Will, will recognize Hashem as the Source of all and will see the sanctity, perfection, and purpose of all He has done.

segments.heText: יִתְגַּדַּל וְיִתְקַדַּשׁ שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא. בְּעָלְמָא דִּי בְרָא כִרְעוּתֵהּ וְיַמְלִיךְ מַלְכוּתֵהּ, בְּחַיֵּיכוֹן וּבְיוֹמֵיכוֹן וּבְחַיֵּי דְכָל בֵּית יִשְׂרָאֵל, בַּעֲגָלָא וּבִזְמַן קָרִיב וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן:

segments.enText: אָמֵן. יְהֵא שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא מְבָרַךְ לְעָלַם וּלְעָלְמֵי עָלְמַיָּא — (We all enthusiastically respond:) Absolutely correct! May everyone (including me!), everywhere, and forever, see that all His actions are great and deserving of praise!

segments.heText: יְהֵא שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא מְבָרַךְ לְעָלַם וּלְעָלְמֵי עָלְמַיָּא: יִתְבָּרַךְ, וְיִשְׁתַּבַּח, וְיִתְפָּאַר, וְיִתְרוֹמַם, וְיִתְנַשֵּׂא, וְיִתְהַדָּר, וְיִתְעַלֶּה, וְיִתְהַלָּל, שְׁמֵהּ דְּקֻדְשָׁא בְּרִיךְ הוּא:

segments.enText: יִתְבָּרַךְ וְיִשְׁתַּבַּח (The person saying Kaddish then points out that) in reality, the true greatness and power of Hashem far exceeds any praises that we mere humans could say. And to that we respond: אָמֵן — Absolutely correct!

segments.heText: לְעֵלָּא מִן כָּל בִּרְכָתָא (בעשי"ת לְעֵלָּא וּלְעֵלָּא מִכָּל בִּרְכָתָא) וְשִׁירָתָא, תֻּשְׁבְּחָתָא וְנֶחָמָתָא, דַּאֲמִירָן בְּעָלְמָא, וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן:
סֵדֶר קְרִיאַת הַתּוֹרָה
segments.enText: Now Let's Learn Some Torah!

Everyone should learn Torah every day—each at their own level! In addition to this, a public reading of the Torah was established for Mondays and Thursdays as a mini "reenactment" of Maamad Har Sinai, so that we may reaffirm the Torah as being our guide—not only personally but also nationally.
Taking Out the Torah

segments.heText: הוֹצָאַת סֵפֶר תּוֹרָה
אֵל אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם / וַיְהִי בִּנְסֹעַ הָאָרֹן / בָּרִיךְ שְׁמֵהּ / גַּדְּלוּ

segments.enText: Said on Mondays and Thursdays when Tachanun was recited:

segments.heText: נוסח פולין
אֵל אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וּמָלֵא רַחֲמִים, אַל תַּסְתֵּר פָּנֶיךָ מִמֶּנּוּ: חוּשָׁה יְהֹוָה עַל יִשְׂרָאֵל עַמֶּךָ וְהַצִּילֵנוּ מִכָּל רָע: חָטָאנוּ לְךָ אָדוֹן, סְלַח נָא כְּרוֹב רַחֲמֶיךָ אֵל:
נוסח אשכנז
אֵל אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וְרַב חֶסֶד וֶאֱמֶת, אַל בְּאַפְּךָ תוֹכִיחֵנוּ: חוּסָה יְהֹוָה עַל יִשְׂרָאֵל עַמֶּךָ וְהוֹשִׁיעֵנוּ מִכָּל רָע: חָטָאנוּ לְךָ אָדוֹן, סְלַח נָא כְּרוֹב רַחֲמֶיךָ אֵל:

segments.enText: אֵל אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם — (As we take out the Torah, we proclaim that) we know that we have not fulfilled everything that it says in Your Torah, but please, in Your loving-kindness, do not rebuke us. Instead, forgive us, and help us see Your Presence in the world.

וַיְהִי בִּנְסֹעַ הָאָרֹן — When the Aron traveled (leading Bnei Yisrael through the desert), וַיֹּאמֶר מֹשֶׁה — Moshe requested that all our enemies and those who hate us should run away in fright! כִּי מִצִּיּוֹן תֵּצֵא תוֹרָה, וּדְבַר יְהֹוָה מִירוּשָׁלָיִם — (So too, when the Beis Hamikdash will be rebuilt, all the nations of the world will come to Yerushalayim to learn the truth and will proclaim:) "Torah comes out of Zion (to light up the entire world) and Hashem's truth comes (only) from Yerushalayim!"

segments.heText: וַיְהִי בִּנְסֹעַ הָאָרֹן וַיֹּאמֶר מֹשֶׁה, קוּמָה יְהֹוָה וְיָפֻצוּ אֹיְבֶיךָ, וְיָנֻסוּ מְשַׂנְאֶיךָ מִפָּנֶיךָ: כִּי מִצִּיּוֹן תֵּצֵא תוֹרָה, וּדְבַר יְהֹוָה מִירוּשָׁלָיִם: בָּרוּךְ שֶׁנָּתַן תּוֹרָה לְעַמּוֹ יִשְׂרָאֵל בִּקְדֻשָּׁתוֹ:

segments.enText: בָּרוּךְ שֶׁנָּתַן תּוֹרָה לְעַמּוֹ יִשְׂרָאֵל בִּקְדֻשָּׁתוֹ — We thank You, Hashem, for giving us Your Torah and trusting us with the responsibility (now—even before Mashiach comes) to spread its truth everywhere (by our actions and words).

segments.heText: בְּרִיךְ שְׁמֵהּ

segments.enText: A tefillah from the Zohar, based on the idea that when the aron kodesh is open, it is an auspicious time to make requests.

בְּרִיךְ שְׁמֵהּ דְּמָרֵא עָלְמָא — Hashem, You are the Source of everything! Please redeem us, rebuild the Beis Hamikdash, shine Your light on us, and accept all our tefillos. May we all live long and happy lives. Protect me, all that is mine, and all that belongs to all of Bnei Yisrael. You feed all, You sustain all, You rule over all—and I am Your servant, who bows before Your glorious Torah! I do not put my trust in humans or in angels, but rather, I place my complete trust in You, for You are the One True God, Whose Torah and Prophets are all true, and Whose actions are all good and true! Please, open my heart to Your Torah! And fulfill all that I and all Your nation Yisrael wish for—but only if what we wish for is really good for us and brings us life and peace.

segments.heText: עָלֵינוּ
segments.enText: Shacharis • Aleinu
We Have a Unique Relationship with Hashem—and We Hope Soon All Mankind Will Also Recognize Hashem

segments.heText: עָלֵינוּ לְשַׁבֵּחַ לַאֲדוֹן הַכֹּל, לָתֵת גְּדֻלָּה לְיוֹצֵר בְּרֵאשִׁית, שֶׁלֹּא עָשָׂנוּ כְּגוֹיֵי הָאֲרָצוֹת, וְלֹא שָׂמָנוּ כְּמִשְׁפְּחוֹת הָאֲדָמָה. שֶׁלֹּא שָׂם חֶלְקֵנוּ כָּהֶם, וְגוֹרָלֵנוּ כְּכָל הֲמוֹנָם, שֶׁהֵם מִשְׁתַּחֲוִים לְהֶבֶל וָרִיק, וּמִתְפַּלְלִים אֶל אֵל לֹא יוֹשִׁיעַ. וַאֲנַחְנוּ כּוֹרְעִים וּמִשְׁתַּחֲוִים וּמוֹדִים, לִפְנֵי מֶלֶךְ מַלְכֵי הַמְּלָכִים הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא. שֶׁהוּא נוֹטֶה שָׁמַיִם וְיוֹסֵד אָרֶץ, וּמוֹשַׁב יְקָרוֹ בַּשָּׁמַיִם מִמַּעַל, וּשְׁכִינַת עֻזּוֹ בְּגָבְהֵי מְרוֹמִים. הוּא אֱלֹהֵינוּ אֵין עוֹד, אֱמֶת מַלְכֵּנוּ, אֶפֶס זוּלָתוֹ. כַּכָּתוּב בְּתוֹרָתוֹ: וְיָדַעְתָּ הַיּוֹם וַהֲשֵׁבֹתָ אֶל לְבָבֶךָ, כִּי יְהוָה הוּא הָאֱלֹהִים בַּשָּׁמַיִם מִמַּעַל וְעַל הָאָרֶץ מִתָּחַת, אֵין עוֹד.

segments.enText: עָלֵינוּ לְשַׁבֵּחַ לַאֲדוֹן הַכֹּל—We feel that it is our obligation to praise Hashem, the Creator of the world, for giving us a unique position among all peoples of the world! We have a very special relationship with Hashem, a unique mission with a clear set of responsibilities and a unique eternal destiny, which no other nation has! They serve gods and powers that are empty and ineffective; וַאֲנַחְנוּ כּוֹרְעִים וּמִשְׁתַּחֲוִים וּמוֹדִים—while we submit ourselves to the King of all Kings; הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא—Who is both our understanding; הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא—His Presence in the Heavens) and at the same time, He is also the Source of everything here on earth (בָּרוּךְ הוּא)! הוּא אֱלֹהֵינוּ אֵין עוֹד—Hashem is OUR personal God, there is no other power that controls us; אֱמֶת מַלְכֵּנוּ, אֶפֶס זוּלָתוֹ—Hashem our King is true, there is nothing beside Him. As the pasuk says: "You must know today and take to heart that Hashem alone is our God; in the Heavens above and on the earth below; אֵין עוֹד—there is nothing else (but Hashem)!"

segments.heText: עַל כֵּן נְקַוֶּה לְּךָ יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ, לִרְאוֹת מְהֵרָה בְּתִפְאֶרֶת עֻזֶּךָ, לְהַעֲבִיר גִּלּוּלִים מִן הָאָרֶץ, וְהָאֱלִילִים כָּרוֹת יִכָּרֵתוּן, לְתַקֵּן עוֹלָם בְּמַלְכוּת שַׁדַּי, וְכָל בְּנֵי בָשָׂר יִקְרְאוּ בִשְׁמֶךָ, לְהַפְנוֹת אֵלֶיךָ כָּל רִשְׁעֵי אָרֶץ. יַכִּירוּ וְיֵדְעוּ כָּל יוֹשְׁבֵי תֵבֵל, כִּי לְךָ תִּכְרַע כָּל בֶּרֶךְ, תִּשָּׁבַע כָּל לָשׁוֹן. לְפָנֶיךָ יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ יִכְרְעוּ וְיִפֹּלוּ, וְלִכְבוֹד שִׁמְךָ יְקָר יִתֵּנוּ, וִיקַבְּלוּ כֻלָּם אֶת עֹל מַלְכוּתֶךָ, וְתִמְלֹךְ עֲלֵיהֶם מְהֵרָה לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד. כִּי הַמַּלְכוּת שֶׁלְּךָ הִיא, וּלְעוֹלְמֵי עַד תִּמְלֹךְ בְּכָבוֹד. כַּכָּתוּב בְּתוֹרָתֶךָ: יְהוָה יִמְלֹךְ לְעֹלָם וָעֶד. וְנֶאֱמַר: וְהָיָה יְהוָה לְמֶלֶךְ עַל כָּל הָאָרֶץ, בַּיּוֹם הַהוּא יִהְיֶה יְהוָה אֶחָד וּשְׁמוֹ אֶחָד.

segments.enText: עַל כֵּן נְקַוֶּה לְּךָ—Therefore, we hope that soon we will see Your power and glory when You destroy all of society's false gods, and all of humanity will recognize Your Presence, and all the wicked will submit to You and accept the yoke of Your Kingdom; and You will rule over the whole world; soon and forever! וְנֶאֱמַר—As the pasuk says: "Then Hashem will be the King over the entire earth; on that day Hashem will be One (everyone will realize there is no other power but Hashem), and His Name will be One (everyone will realize that all that has happened since creation is all good, and all came from Hashem as part of one unified plan)."

segments.heText: אַל תִּירָא מִפַּחַד פִּתְאֹם וּמִשֹּׁאַת רְשָׁעִים כִּי תָבֹא. עֻצוּ עֵצָה וְתֻפָר, דַּבְּרוּ דָבָר וְלֹא יָקוּם, כִּי עִמָּנוּ אֵל. וְעַד זִקְנָה אֲנִי הוּא, וְעַד שֵׂיבָה אֲנִי אֶסְבֹּל, אֲנִי עָשִׂיתִי וַאֲנִי אֶשָּׂא, וַאֲנִי אֶסְבֹּל וַאֲמַלֵּט.
שִׁיר שֶׁל יוֹם
segments.enText: Shir Shel Yom
Each Day Is Unique and Gets Us Closer to Shabbos—and Closer to Mashiach!

Each day, as part of the avodah in the Beis Hamikdash, the Leviim sang a different "song" (chapter of Tehillim) that (according to the Gemara) represents that day's aspect of Creation. In the hope that soon the Beis Hamikdash will be rebuilt, we also recite songs every day. By counting the days in reference to Shabbos (בְּשַׁבָּת, יוֹם רִאשׁוֹן, etc.), we fulfill the mitzvah to remember the Shabbos every day. In addition, this ties our daily successes to Shabbos, rather than to the planets (which is the source of the non-Jewish names of each day). According to the Zohar, each chapter connects to a different millennium of the seven one-thousand-year periods from Creation, thus each "day" gets us closer to "Shabbos"—שַׁבָּת שֶׁל שַׁבָּת—the coming of Mashiach.

segments.heText: שִׁיר שֶׁל יוֹם רִאשׁוֹן
segments.enText: Sunday (Chapter 24)

segments.heText: הַיּוֹם, יוֹם רִאשׁוֹן בְּשַׁבָּת, שֶׁבּוֹ הָיוּ הַלְוִיִּם אוֹמְרִים בְּבֵית הַמִּקְדָּשׁ:

segments.enText: לְדָוִד מִזְמוֹר—The entire earth belongs to Hashem. Therefore, only those who are sincere in the service of Hashem, who use this material world for noble goals and who deal with others with honesty and integrity! We hope for the day when all of civilization will recognize Hashem and live by these values.

segments.heText: לְדָוִד מִזְמוֹר, לַיהוה הָאָרֶץ וּמְלוֹאָהּ, תֵּבֵל וְיֹשְׁבֵי בָהּ: כִּי הוּא עַל יַמִּים יְסָדָהּ, וְעַל נְהָרוֹת יְכוֹנְנֶהָ: מִי יַעֲלֶה בְהַר יהוה, וּמִי יָקוּם בִּמְקוֹם קָדְשׁוֹ: נְקִי כַפַּיִם וּבַר לֵבָב, אֲשֶׁר לֹא נָשָׂא לַשָּׁוְא נַפְשִׁי וְלֹא נִשְׁבַּע לְמִרְמָה: יִשָּׂא בְרָכָה מֵאֵת יהוה, וּצְדָקָה מֵאֱלֹהֵי יִשְׁעוֹ: זֶה דּוֹר דֹּרְשָׁיו, מְבַקְשֵׁי פָנֶיךָ יַעֲקֹב סֶלָה: שְׂאוּ שְׁעָרִים רָאשֵׁיכֶם, וְהִנָּשְׂאוּ פִּתְחֵי עוֹלָם, וְיָבוֹא מֶלֶךְ הַכָּבוֹד: מִי זֶה מֶלֶךְ הַכָּבוֹד, יהוה עִזּוּז וְגִבּוֹר, יהוה גִּבּוֹר מִלְחָמָה: שְׂאוּ שְׁעָרִים רָאשֵׁיכֶם, וּשְׂאוּ פִּתְחֵי עוֹלָם, וְיָבֹא מֶלֶךְ הַכָּבוֹד: מִי הוּא זֶה מֶלֶךְ הַכָּבוֹד, יהוה צְבָאוֹת הוּא מֶלֶךְ הַכָּבוֹד סֶלָה:
קַדִּישׁ יָתוֹם
שִׁיר שֶׁל יוֹם שֵׁנִי
segments.enText: Monday (Chapter 48)

segments.heText: הַיּוֹם, יוֹם שֵׁנִי בְּשַׁבָּת, שֶׁבּוֹ הָיוּ הַלְוִיִּם אוֹמְרִים בְּבֵית הַמִּקְדָּשׁ:

segments.enText: שִׁיר מִזְמוֹר לִבְנֵי קֹרַח—The city of Yerushalayim was chosen by Hashem as His special place where He will reveal His greatness to the nations of the world. And just as Yerushalayim (with its unique kedushah and beauty) is to be an example to us of how to conduct our lives, so, too, we are to be a model for others to emulate. And as the unique status of

segments.heText: שִׁיר מִזְמוֹר לִבְנֵי קֹרַח, גָּדוֹל יהוה וּמְהֻלָּל מְאֹד, בְּעִיר אֱלֹהֵינוּ הַר קָדְשׁוֹ: יְפֵה נוֹף מְשׂוֹשׂ כָּל הָאָרֶץ הַר צִיּוֹן יַרְכְּתֵי צָפוֹן, קִרְיַת מֶלֶךְ רָב: אֱלֹהִים בְּאַרְמְנוֹתֶיהָ נוֹדַע לְמִשְׂגָּב: כִּי הִנֵּה הַמְּלָכִים נוֹעֲדוּ, עָבְרוּ יַחְדָּו: הֵמָּה רָאוּ כֵּן תָּמָהוּ, נִבְהֲלוּ נֶחְפָּזוּ: רְעָדָה אֲחָזָתַם שָׁם, חִיל כַּיּוֹלֵדָה: בְּרוּחַ קָדִים תְּשַׁבֵּר אֳנִיּוֹת תַּרְשִׁישׁ: כַּאֲשֶׁר שָׁמַעְנוּ כֵּן רָאִינוּ בְּעִיר יהוה צְבָאוֹת בְּעִיר אֱלֹהֵינוּ, אֱלֹהִים יְכוֹנְנֶהָ עַד עוֹלָם סֶלָה: דִּמִּינוּ אֱלֹהִים חַסְדֶּךָ, בְּקֶרֶב הֵיכָלֶךָ: כְּשִׁמְךָ אֱלֹהִים כֵּן תְּהִלָּתְךָ עַל קַצְוֵי אֶרֶץ, צֶדֶק מָלְאָה יְמִינֶךָ: יִשְׂמַח הַר צִיּוֹן תָּגֵלְנָה בְּנוֹת יְהוּדָה, לְמַעַן מִשְׁפָּטֶיךָ: סֹבּוּ צִיּוֹן וְהַקִּיפוּהָ, סִפְרוּ מִגְדָּלֶיהָ: שִׁיתוּ לִבְּכֶם לְחֵילָה, פַּסְּגוּ אַרְמְנוֹתֶיהָ, לְמַעַן תְּסַפְּרוּ לְדוֹר אַחֲרוֹן: כִּי זֶה אֱלֹהִים אֱלֹהֵינוּ עוֹלָם וָעֶד, הוּא יְנַהֲגֵנוּ עַל מוּת:

segments.enText: Yerushalayim is eternal, so, too, our unique relationship with Hashem is eternal.

segments.heText: קַדִּישׁ יָתוֹם
שִׁיר שֶׁל יוֹם שְׁלִישִׁי
segments.enText: Tuesday (Chapter 82)

segments.heText: הַיּוֹם, יוֹם שְׁלִישִׁי בְּשַׁבָּת, שֶׁבּוֹ הָיוּ הַלְוִיִּם אוֹמְרִים בְּבֵית הַמִּקְדָּשׁ:

segments.enText: מִזְמוֹר לְאָסָף—The continued existence of the world is dependent on justice and the fair application of laws. Since justice is ultimately in the Hands of Hashem—all judges—including all of us who are constantly judging our fellow men—must act with honesty and integrity. Our opinions and decisions (about how we treat people) have a great effect on the world.

segments.heText: מִזְמוֹר לְאָסָף, אֱלֹהִים נִצָּב בַּעֲדַת אֵל, בְּקֶרֶב אֱלֹהִים יִשְׁפֹּט: עַד מָתַי תִּשְׁפְּטוּ עָוֶל, וּפְנֵי רְשָׁעִים תִּשְׂאוּ סֶלָה: שִׁפְטוּ דָל וְיָתוֹם, עָנִי וָרָשׁ הַצְדִּיקוּ: פַּלְּטוּ דַל וְאֶבְיוֹן, מִיַּד רְשָׁעִים הַצִּילוּ: לֹא יָדְעוּ וְלֹא יָבִינוּ, בַּחֲשֵׁכָה יִתְהַלָּכוּ, יִמּוֹטוּ כָּל מוֹסְדֵי אָרֶץ: אֲנִי אָמַרְתִּי אֱלֹהִים אַתֶּם, וּבְנֵי עֶלְיוֹן כֻּלְּכֶם: אָכֵן כְּאָדָם תְּמוּתוּן, וּכְאַחַד הַשָּׂרִים תִּפֹּלוּ: קוּמָה אֱלֹהִים שָׁפְטָה הָאָרֶץ, כִּי אַתָּה תִנְחַל בְּכָל הַגּוֹיִם:
קַדִּישׁ יָתוֹם
שִׁיר שֶׁל יוֹם רְבִיעִי
segments.enText: Wednesday (Chapter 94)

segments.heText: הַיּוֹם, יוֹם רְבִיעִי בְּשַׁבָּת, שֶׁבּוֹ הָיוּ הַלְוִיִּם אוֹמְרִים בְּבֵית הַמִּקְדָּשׁ:

segments.enText: אֵל נְקָמוֹת—How long will the forces of evil be victorious? They trample Bnei Yisrael, oppressing widows and orphans, claiming that You, Hashem, do not see or care! How foolish are they to think that way! Hashem, judge them and repay them with what they deserve. We understand that all You do to us as a nation and individually is for our good. You will never abandon us, and therefore we sing Your praises as our great King Who controls everything.

segments.heText: אֵל נְקָמוֹת יהוה, אֵל נְקָמוֹת הוֹפִיעַ: הִנָּשֵׂא שֹׁפֵט הָאָרֶץ, הָשֵׁב גְּמוּל עַל גֵּאִים: עַד מָתַי רְשָׁעִים יהוה, עַד מָתַי רְשָׁעִים יַעֲלֹזוּ: יַבִּיעוּ יְדַבְּרוּ עָתָק, יִתְאַמְּרוּ כָּל פֹּעֲלֵי אָוֶן: עַמְּךָ יהוה יְדַכְּאוּ, וְנַחֲלָתְךָ יְעַנּוּ: אַלְמָנָה וְגֵר יַהֲרֹגוּ, וִיתוֹמִים יְרַצֵּחוּ: וַיֹּאמְרוּ לֹא יִרְאֶה יָּהּ, וְלֹא יָבִין אֱלֹהֵי יַעֲקֹב: בִּינוּ בֹּעֲרִים בָּעָם, וּכְסִילִים מָתַי תַּשְׂכִּילוּ: הֲנֹטַע אֹזֶן הֲלֹא יִשְׁמָע, אִם יֹצֵר עַיִן הֲלֹא יַבִּיט: הֲיֹסֵר גּוֹיִם הֲלֹא יוֹכִיחַ, הַמְלַמֵּד אָדָם דָּעַת: יהוה יֹדֵעַ מַחְשְׁבוֹת אָדָם, כִּי הֵמָּה הָבֶל: אַשְׁרֵי הַגֶּבֶר אֲשֶׁר תְּיַסְּרֶנּוּ יָּהּ, וּמִתּוֹרָתְךָ תְלַמְּדֶנּוּ: לְהַשְׁקִיט לוֹ מִימֵי רָע, עַד יִכָּרֶה לָרָשָׁע שָׁחַת: כִּי לֹא יִטֹּשׁ יהוה עַמּוֹ, וְנַחֲלָתוֹ לֹא יַעֲזֹב: כִּי עַד צֶדֶק יָשׁוּב מִשְׁפָּט, וְאַחֲרָיו כָּל יִשְׁרֵי לֵב: מִי יָקוּם לִי עִם מְרֵעִים, מִי יִתְיַצֵּב לִי עִם פֹּעֲלֵי אָוֶן: לוּלֵי יהוה עֶזְרָתָה לִּי, כִּמְעַט שָׁכְנָה דוּמָה נַפְשִׁי: אִם אָמַרְתִּי מָטָה רַגְלִי, חַסְדְּךָ יהוה יִסְעָדֵנִי: בְּרֹב שַׂרְעַפַּי בְּקִרְבִּי, תַּנְחוּמֶיךָ יְשַׁעַשְׁעוּ נַפְשִׁי: הַיְחָבְרְךָ כִּסֵּא הַוּוֹת, יֹצֵר עָמָל עֲלֵי חֹק: יָגוֹדּוּ עַל נֶפֶשׁ צַדִּיק, וְדָם נָקִי יַרְשִׁיעוּ: וַיְהִי יהוה לִי לְמִשְׂגָּב, וֵאלֹהַי לְצוּר מַחְסִי: וַיָּשֶׁב עֲלֵיהֶם אֶת אוֹנָם וּבְרָעָתָם יַצְמִיתֵם, יַצְמִיתֵם יהוה אֱלֹהֵינוּ:
קַדִּישׁ יָתוֹם

segments.heText: תהילים כז

segments.heText: לְדָוִד ה' אוֹרִי
segments.enText: Just Ask Hashem for Help in Your Teshuvah Process—His Light Is There If You Look for It!

segments.heText: לְדָוִד יְהוָה אוֹרִי וְיִשְׁעִי
segments.enText: Hashem is always there for me, He is my guiding light and my Source of strength! Therefore, I have nothing to fear in life, not human enemies and physical war, nor spiritual battles and the yetzer hara, because שְׁאֵלְתִּי מֵאֵת יְהוָה אוֹתָהּ אֲבַקֵּשׁ (though I have asked Hashem for all sorts of things that I feel I need, in truth) I have one overall goal for my life: שִׁבְתִּי בְּבֵית יְהוָה כָּל יְמֵי חַיַּי—לַחֲזוֹת בְּנֹעַם יְהוָה וּלְבַקֵּר בְּהֵיכָלוֹ—that "my house," meaning, all that I do and how I run my life, should be based on the values of Torah, thus making "my house" a house of Hashem. (Since I have total free will over my moral choices, no enemy can rob me of my ability to create a Torah life, no matter what my circumstances are! Therefore, by trusting in Hashem and following His light) I am safe and will always be able to rise above my physical and spiritual enemies.

segments.heText: אַל תַּסְתֵּר פָּנֶיךָ מִמֶּנִּי
segments.enText: Please, Hashem, do not hide Your Presence and light from me! הוֹרֵנִי יְהוָה דַּרְכֶּךָ—Show me the right path and help me make the correct decisions.

segments.heText: קַוֵּה אֶל יְהוָה
segments.enText: (Proclaims David HaMelech:) "Put your trust in Hashem; (and) חֲזַק וְיַאֲמֵץ לִבֶּךָ that itself will give you the courage and strength to persevere! (And if Hashem has not yet given you what you asked for;) וְקַוֵּה אֶל יְהוָה—continue to place your trust in Hashem (for all that He does is for your best)."

segments.heText: לְדָוִד, יְהוָה אוֹרִי וְיִשְׁעִי מִמִּי אִירָא, יְהוָה מָעוֹז חַיַּי מִמִּי אֶפְחָד: בִּקְרֹב עָלַי מְרֵעִים לֶאֱכֹל אֶת בְּשָׂרִי, צָרַי וְאֹיְבַי לִי, הֵמָּה כָשְׁלוּ וְנָפָלוּ: אִם תַּחֲנֶה עָלַי מַחֲנֶה לֹא יִירָא לִבִּי, אִם תָּקוּם עָלַי מִלְחָמָה בְּזֹאת אֲנִי בוֹטֵחַ: אַחַת שָׁאַלְתִּי מֵאֵת יְהוָה אוֹתָהּ אֲבַקֵּשׁ, שִׁבְתִּי בְּבֵית יְהוָה כָּל יְמֵי חַיַּי, לַחֲזוֹת בְּנֹעַם יְהוָה וּלְבַקֵּר בְּהֵיכָלוֹ: כִּי יִצְפְּנֵנִי בְּסֻכֹּה בְּיוֹם רָעָה, יַסְתִּרֵנִי בְּסֵתֶר אָהֳלוֹ, בְּצוּר יְרוֹמְמֵנִי: וְעַתָּה יָרוּם רֹאשִׁי עַל אֹיְבַי סְבִיבוֹתַי, וְאֶזְבְּחָה בְאָהֳלוֹ זִבְחֵי תְרוּעָה, אָשִׁירָה וַאֲזַמְּרָה לַיהוָה: שְׁמַע יְהוָה קוֹלִי אֶקְרָא, וְחָנֵּנִי וַעֲנֵנִי: לְךָ אָמַר לִבִּי בַּקְּשׁוּ פָנָי, אֶת פָּנֶיךָ יְהוָה אֲבַקֵּשׁ: אַל תַּסְתֵּר פָּנֶיךָ מִמֶּנִּי, אַל תַּט בְּאַף עַבְדֶּךָ, עֶזְרָתִי הָיִיתָ, אַל תִּטְּשֵׁנִי וְאַל תַּעַזְבֵנִי אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׁעִי: כִּי אָבִי וְאִמִּי עֲזָבוּנִי, וַיהוָה יַאַסְפֵנִי: הוֹרֵנִי יְהוָה דַּרְכֶּךָ, וּנְחֵנִי בְּאֹרַח מִישׁוֹר לְמַעַן שׁוֹרְרָי: אַל תִּתְּנֵנִי בְּנֶפֶשׁ צָרָי, כִּי קָמוּ בִי עֵדֵי שֶׁקֶר וִיפֵחַ חָמָס: לוּלֵא הֶאֱמַנְתִּי לִרְאוֹת בְּטוּב יְהוָה בְּאֶרֶץ חַיִּים: קַוֵּה אֶל יְהוָה, חֲזַק וְיַאֲמֵץ לִבֶּךָ, וְקַוֵּה אֶל יְהוָה:
קדיש יתום
segments.enText: Mourner's Kaddish
In Israel, the following is added:

As we transition to our work day, we focus on: (1) trusting in Hashem that only He can make all our efforts succeed; (2) reciting the Ketores which, according to Kabbalistic sources, brings spiritual sustenance to ourselves and the world around us for the rest of the day; (3) the importance of using halacha to guide all our activities; and (notwithstanding the competitive nature of school and work) (4) the importance of peace and harmony.

קַוֵּה אֶל יְהֹוָה—Put your trust in Hashem; (and) חֲזַק וְיַאֲמֵץ לִבֶּךָ—that itself will give you the courage and strength to persevere! (And if Hashem has not given you what you asked for); קַוֵּה אֶל יְהֹוָה—continue to place your trust in Hashem (for there is no other power to rely on but Hashem alone)!

segments.heText: קַוֵּה אֶל יְהֹוָה, חֲזַק וְיַאֲמֵץ לִבֶּךָ, וְקַוֵּה אֶל יְהֹוָה: אֵין קָדוֹשׁ כַּיהֹוָה, כִּי אֵין בִּלְתֶּךָ, וְאֵין צוּר כֵּאלֹהֵינוּ: כִּי מִי אֱלוֹהַּ מִבַּלְעֲדֵי יְהֹוָה, וּמִי צוּר זוּלָתִי אֱלֹהֵינוּ:

segments.enText: אֵין כֵּאלֹהֵינוּ—There is no other power like Hashem our King! Therefore, we thank You, for You are the Source of all blessing and salvation. And it was to You alone that (in the times of the Beis Hamikdash) we offered the Ketores.

segments.heText: אֵין כֵּאלֹהֵינוּ, אֵין כַּאדוֹנֵינוּ, אֵין כְּמַלְכֵּנוּ, אֵין כְּמוֹשִׁיעֵנוּ: מִי כֵאלֹהֵינוּ, מִי כַאדוֹנֵינוּ, מִי כְמַלְכֵּנוּ, מִי כְמוֹשִׁיעֵנוּ: נוֹדֶה לֵאלֹהֵינוּ, נוֹדֶה לַאדוֹנֵינוּ, נוֹדֶה לְמַלְכֵּנוּ, נוֹדֶה לְמוֹשִׁיעֵנוּ: בָּרוּךְ אֱלֹהֵינוּ, בָּרוּךְ אֲדוֹנֵינוּ, בָּרוּךְ מַלְכֵּנוּ, בָּרוּךְ מוֹשִׁיעֵנוּ: אַתָּה הוּא אֱלֹהֵינוּ, אַתָּה הוּא אֲדוֹנֵינוּ, אַתָּה הוּא מַלְכֵּנוּ, אַתָּה הוּא מוֹשִׁיעֵנוּ: אַתָּה הוּא שֶׁהִקְטִירוּ אֲבוֹתֵינוּ לְפָנֶיךָ אֶת קְטֹרֶת הַסַּמִּים:

segments.enText: פִּטּוּם הַקְּטֹרֶת—A section from Maseches Kerisos describing in detail the exact ingredients of the Ketores, how the Ketores was made, and what invalidated the Ketores.

segments.heText: פִּטּוּם הַקְּטֹרֶת. הַצֳּרִי, וְהַצִּפֹּרֶן, הַחֶלְבְּנָה, וְהַלְּבוֹנָה, מִשְׁקַל שִׁבְעִים שִׁבְעִים מָנֶה, מוֹר, וּקְצִיעָה, שִׁבֹּלֶת נֵרְדְּ, וְכַרְכֹּם, מִשְׁקַל שִׁשָּׁה עָשָׂר שִׁשָּׁה עָשָׂר מָנֶה, הַקֹּשְׁטְ שְׁנֵים עָשָׂר, וְקִלּוּפָה שְׁלֹשָׁה, וְקִנָּמוֹן תִּשְׁעָה, בֹּרִית כַּרְשִׁינָה תִּשְׁעָה קַבִּין, יֵין קַפְרִיסִין סְאִין תְּלָתָא וְקַבִּין תְּלָתָא, וְאִם אֵין לוֹ יֵין קַפְרִיסִין מֵבִיא חֲמַר חִוַּרְיָן עַתִּיק, מֶלַח סְדוֹמִית רוֹבַע, מַעֲלֶה עָשָׁן כָּל שֶׁהוּא. רַבִּי נָתָן הַבַּבְלִי אוֹמֵר: אַף כִּפַּת הַיַּרְדֵּן כָּל שֶׁהוּא, וְאִם נָתַן בָּהּ דְּבַשׁ פְּסָלָהּ, וְאִם חִסַּר אַחַת מִכָּל סַמָּנֶיהָ חַיָּב מִיתָה: רַבָּן שִׁמְעוֹן בֶּן גַּמְלִיאֵל אוֹמֵר: הַצֳּרִי אֵינוֹ אֶלָּא שְׂרָף הַנּוֹטֵף מֵעֲצֵי הַקְּטָף. בֹּרִית כַּרְשִׁינָה שֶׁשָּׁפִין בָּהּ אֶת הַצִּפֹּרֶן כְּדֵי שֶׁתְּהֵא נָאָה, יֵין קַפְרִיסִין שֶׁשּׁוֹרִין בּוֹ אֶת הַצִּפֹּרֶן כְּדֵי שֶׁתְּהֵא עַזָּה: וַהֲלֹא מֵי רַגְלַיִם יָפִין לָהּ, אֶלָּא שֶׁאֵין מַכְנִיסִין מֵי רַגְלַיִם בַּמִּקְדָּשׁ מִפְּנֵי הַכָּבוֹד:

segments.enText: תָּנָא דְּבֵי אֵלִיָּהוּ—In the beis medrash of Eliyahu it was taught: Anyone who studies halachah every day is assured a place in Olam Haba!

segments.heText: תָּנָא דְּבֵי אֵלִיָּהוּ, כָּל הַשּׁוֹנֶה הֲלָכוֹת בְּכָל יוֹם מֻבְטָח לוֹ שֶׁהוּא בֶּן עוֹלָם הַבָּא, שֶׁנֶּאֱמַר: הֲלִיכוֹת עוֹלָם לוֹ. אַל תִּקְרֵי הֲלִיכוֹת אֶלָּא הֲלָכוֹת:

segments.enText: אָמַר רַבִּי אֶלְעָזָר—Said Rabbi Elazar in the name of Rabbi Chanina: Talmidei chachamim increase peace in the world. Those who love Torah will have peace (will not fear from their enemies), and the yetzer hara will not place stumbling blocks in their path. May there be peace (from wars) in Yerushalayim, serenity, and calm within its inhabitants. May the Beis Hamikdash be rebuilt, and may Hashem give all of Bnei Yisrael strength (to conquer our spiritual and physical enemies) and grant us all (inner and outer) peace!

segments.heText: אָמַר רַבִּי אֶלְעָזָר אָמַר רַבִּי חֲנִינָא, תַּלְמִידֵי חֲכָמִים מַרְבִּים שָׁלוֹם בָּעוֹלָם, שֶׁנֶּאֱמַר: וְכָל בָּנַיִךְ לִמּוּדֵי יְהֹוָה וְרַב שְׁלוֹם בָּנָיִךְ: אַל תִּקְרֵי בָּנָיִךְ אֶלָּא בּוֹנָיִךְ: שָׁלוֹם רָב לְאֹהֲבֵי תוֹרָתֶךָ, וְאֵין לָמוֹ מִכְשׁוֹל: יְהִי שָׁלוֹם בְּחֵילֵךְ, שַׁלְוָה בְּאַרְמְנוֹתָיִךְ: לְמַעַן אַחַי וְרֵעָי אֲדַבְּרָה נָּא שָׁלוֹם בָּךְ: לְמַעַן בֵּית יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ אֲבַקְשָׁה טוֹב לָךְ: יְהֹוָה עֹז לְעַמּוֹ יִתֵּן, יְהֹוָה יְבָרֵךְ אֶת עַמּוֹ בַשָּׁלוֹם:
שֵׁשׁ זְכִירוֹת
segments.enText: As You Go through Your Day, Don't Forget!

Though there are siddurim that list four, and some list ten, this list of six "remembrances" is the most common custom. Note that there are three positive elements to focus on: Hashem redeemed us from Egypt (and will in the future); Hashem spoke directly to us at Har Sinai, and Shabbos, which reminds us of Creation (and thus His providence). The three negative elements tell us to always be on guard against: the Golden Calf (sins against Hashem); Miriam's lashon hara (sins against others); and Amalek (nations who do not fear Hashem, no matter how seemingly "cultured," can act with great cruelty). The point is not to mumble them like some magic formula! Rather, the idea is to focus on these key points and use them as your guide during your day.

segments.heText: לְמַעַן תִּזְכֹּר אֶת יוֹם צֵאתְךָ מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם כֹּל יְמֵי חַיֶּיךָ:

segments.enText: יְצִיאַת מִצְרַיִם—We must remember that Hashem took us out of Egypt with great miracles, which gives us hope that He will again redeem us and take us out of this final galus!

segments.heText: רַק הִשָּׁמֶר לְךָ וּשְׁמֹר נַפְשְׁךָ מְאֹד פֶּן תִּשְׁכַּח אֶת הַדְּבָרִים אֲשֶׁר רָאוּ עֵינֶיךָ וּפֶן יָסוּרוּ מִלְּבָבְךָ כֹּל יְמֵי חַיֶּיךָ וְהוֹדַעְתָּם לְבָנֶיךָ וְלִבְנֵי בָנֶיךָ: יוֹם אֲשֶׁר עָמַדְתָּ לִפְנֵי יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ בְּחֹרֵב:

segments.enText: מַעֲמַד הַר סִינַי—We must remember the unique defining moment of Bnei Yisrael, when Hashem spoke to three million of us at Har Sinai, and gave us His Torah.

segments.heText: זָכוֹר אֵת אֲשֶׁר עָשָׂה לְךָ עֲמָלֵק בַּדֶּרֶךְ בְּצֵאתְכֶם מִמִּצְרָיִם: אֲשֶׁר קָרְךָ בַּדֶּרֶךְ וַיְזַנֵּב בְּךָ כָּל הַנֶּחֱשָׁלִים אַחֲרֶיךָ וְאַתָּה עָיֵף וְיָגֵעַ וְלֹא יָרֵא אֱלֹהִים: וְהָיָה בְּהָנִיחַ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ לְךָ מִכָּל אֹיְבֶיךָ מִסָּבִיב בָּאָרֶץ אֲשֶׁר יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ נֹתֵן לְךָ נַחֲלָה לְרִשְׁתָּהּ תִּמְחֶה אֶת זֵכֶר עֲמָלֵק מִתַּחַת הַשָּׁמָיִם לֹא תִּשְׁכָּח:

segments.enText: מַעֲשֵׂה עֲמָלֵק—We must remember the evil of Amalek attacking us for no reason right after the miracle of the splitting of the sea. We are obligated to wipe out evil.

segments.heText: זְכֹר אַל תִּשְׁכַּח אֵת אֲשֶׁר הִקְצַפְתָּ אֶת יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ בַּמִּדְבָּר:

segments.enText: מַעֲשֵׂה הָעֵגֶל—We must remember how we angered Hashem when we attempted to serve Him our own way, rather than as prescribed by His Torah, notwithstanding all our good intentions!

segments.heText: זָכוֹר אֵת אֲשֶׁר עָשָׂה יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ לְמִרְיָם בַּדֶּרֶךְ בְּצֵאתְכֶם מִמִּצְרָיִם:

segments.enText: מִרְיָם—We must remember what Hashem did to Miriam: Her punishment for speaking lashon hara about Moshe, even though it was a seemingly minor comment, and the great reward she was given (that the entire nation waited for her for seven days) for her seemingly minor chessed that she waited to see what would happen to baby Moshe while he was hidden in a basket in the water.

segments.heText: זָכוֹר אֶת יוֹם הַשַּׁבָּת לְקַדְּשׁוֹ:

segments.enText: שַׁבָּת—We must remember that it is the power and sanctity of Shabbos that gives us the "energy" to remain spiritually strong during the week.

segments.heText: שְׁלֹשָׁה עָשָׂר עִקָּרִים
segments.enText: No Matter What Happens, No Matter Where We Are, This Is What We Believe!

What's popular is not always right, and what's right is not always popular! Not only must we make sure to "do" what is correct, but we need to also stay firm in our core beliefs and value system, which, in many cases, is not popular and are the opposite of what the "world out there" believes. As with the Six Remembrances, the point is not to mumble the Thirteen Principles like some magic formula! Rather, the idea is to think about what they mean to you and to use them as your guide during your day. The commentaries have pointed out that they break down into three basic categories: belief in Hashem (1-5), the truth of Torah (6-9), and our responsibilities as Bnei Yisrael and our ultimate reward (10-13).
I believe with perfect faith that Hashem created everything and guides all creations.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁהַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ הוּא בּוֹרֵא וּמַנְהִיג לְכָל הַבְּרוּאִים, וְהוּא לְבַדּוֹ עָשָׂה וְעוֹשֶׂה וְיַעֲשֶׂה לְכָל הַמַּעֲשִׂים.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that Hashem is One and Unique and that He alone is our God.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁהַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ הוּא יָחִיד וְאֵין יְחִידוּת כָּמוֹהוּ בְּשׁוּם פָּנִים, וְהוּא לְבַדּוֹ אֱלֹהֵינוּ, הָיָה הֹוֶה וְיִהְיֶה.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that Hashem is not physical at all.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁהַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ אֵינוֹ גוּף, וְלֹא יַשִּׂיגוּהוּ מַשִּׂיגֵי הַגּוּף, וְאֵין לוֹ שׁוּם דִּמְיוֹן כְּלָל.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that Hashem is eternal; He is first and last.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁהַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ הוּא רִאשׁוֹן וְהוּא אַחֲרוֹן.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that it is only to Hashem (and directly to Him) that we should pray.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁהַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ לוֹ לְבַדּוֹ רָאוּי לְהִתְפַּלֵּל, וְאֵין רָאוּי לְהִתְפַּלֵּל לְזוּלָתוֹ.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that all the words of the Neviim are true.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁכָּל דִּבְרֵי נְבִיאִים אֱמֶת.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that the prophecy of Moshe was true, and that he was the chief of all the prophets.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁנְּבוּאַת מֹשֶׁה רַבֵּנוּ עָלָיו הַשָּׁלוֹם הָיְתָה אֲמִתִּית, וְשֶׁהוּא הָיָה אָב לַנְּבִיאִים, לַקּוֹדְמִים לְפָנָיו וְלַבָּאִים אַחֲרָיו.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that the entire Torah that we have now is the exact same Torah that was given to Moshe.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁכָּל הַתּוֹרָה הַמְּצוּיָה עַתָּה בְּיָדֵינוּ הִיא הַנְּתוּנָה לְמֹשֶׁה רַבֵּנוּ עָלָיו הַשָּׁלוֹם.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that the Torah is eternal and unchanging.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁזֹּאת הַתּוֹרָה לֹא תְהֵא מֻחְלֶפֶת וְלֹא תְהֵא תוֹרָה אַחֶרֶת מֵאֵת הַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that Hashem knows everything we do and think.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁהַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ יוֹדֵעַ כָּל מַעֲשֵׂה בְנֵי אָדָם וְכָל מַחְשְׁבוֹתָם, שֶׁנֶּאֱמַר: הַיֹּצֵר יַחַד לִבָּם הַמֵּבִין אֶל כָּל מַעֲשֵׂיהֶם.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that Hashem rewards and punishes according to our actions.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁהַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ גּוֹמֵל טוֹב לְשׁוֹמְרֵי מִצְוֹתָיו וּמַעֲנִישׁ לְעוֹבְרֵי מִצְוֹתָיו.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that the Mashiach will come, and I await his arrival every day!

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, בְּבִיאַת הַמָּשִׁיחַ, וְאַף עַל פִּי שֶׁיִּתְמַהְמֵהַּ, עִם כָּל זֶה אֲחַכֶּה לּוֹ בְּכָל יוֹם שֶׁיָּבוֹא.

segments.enText: I believe with perfect faith that there will be Techiyas Hameisim.

segments.heText: אֲנִי מַאֲמִין בֶּאֱמוּנָה שְׁלֵמָה, שֶׁתִּהְיֶה תְּחִיַּת הַמֵּתִים בְּעֵת שֶׁיַּעֲלֶה רָצוֹן מֵאֵת הַבּוֹרֵא יִתְבָּרַךְ שְׁמוֹ וְיִתְעַלֶּה זִכְרוֹ לָעַד וּלְנֵצַח נְצָחִים.
לִישׁוּעָתְךָ קִוִּיתִי יְהֹוָה: קִוִּיתִי יְהֹוָה לִישׁוּעָתְךָ: יְהֹוָה לִישׁוּעָתְךָ קִוִּיתִי: לִפוּרְקָנָךְ סַבְרִית יְהֹוָה: סַבְרִית יְהֹוָה לִפוּרְקָנָךְ: יְהֹוָה לִפוּרְקָנָךְ סַבְרִית:

segments.enText: For Your salvation I hope, Hashem! I hope, Hashem, for Your salvation! Hashem, for Your salvation I hope!

The context of "קִוִּיתִי יְהֹוָה לִישׁוּעָתְךָ—For Your salvation I hope, Hashem!" is the conclusion of Yaakov Avinu's brachah to Dan. Yaakov blessed the tribe of Dan, and specifically their famous descendant Shimshon, with great military strength and cunning, and that the shevet as a whole and Shimshon in particular should be victorious in the many battles against the Philistines. Nevertheless, Yaakov concluded his brachah by proclaiming that it is only "for Your salvation I hope, Hashem." No matter how strong we are and no matter how great our material blessings (and no matter how many of this morning's requests Hashem grants us!), we must put our trust only in Hashem, and never forget that our success is in His Hands!
